content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/EnvironmentEditor/components/EnvironmentFormActions.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 87, 91
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 87):
   86              type="submit"
   87              variant="primary"
   88            >

  (line 91):
   90            </Button>
   91            <Button onClick={onReload} type="button" variant="secondary">
   92              {t('Reload')}

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 82, 91
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ActionGroup

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 82):
   81          <ActionGroup className="pf-v5-c-form">
   82            <Button
   83              isDisabled={isSaveDisabled || loading}

  (line 91):
   90            </Button>
   91            <Button onClick={onReload} type="button" variant="secondary">
   92              {t('Reload')}

```

### Fix 3
Lines: 58, 80
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: StackItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Stack

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: StackItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 58):
   57      <Stack className="environment-form__buttons">
   58        <StackItem>
   59          {(error || apiError) && (

  (line 80):
   79        </StackItem>
   80        <StackItem>
   81          <ActionGroup className="pf-v5-c-form">

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/EnvironmentEditor/components/EnvironmentFormActions.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1: `Button.variant` type changed
Lines 87 and 91 use `variant="primary"` and `variant="secondary"`. The type change is that `'stateful'` was **added** to the union. Both `"primary"` and `"secondary"` are still valid values in the new type. **No change needed** — the existing values are still valid.

### Fix 2: Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
Lines 82 and 91. Looking at the actual JSX:
- Line 82-90: `<Button ...>{t('Save')}</Button>` — children is just text, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 91-93: `<Button ...>{t('Reload')}</Button>` — children is just text, no icon. **No change needed.**

Neither Button contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The rule fires at the ActionGroup parent level but neither Button actually has icon children.

### Fix 3: `StackItem` new `component` prop
Lines 58 and 80. The `component` prop on `StackItem` in PF v6 is actually an HTML element type prop (like `"div"`, `"span"`, etc.), not a content prop. Looking at the rule description more carefully — it says the type is `React.ReactNode`, but in practice, the `StackItem` children here are:
- Line 58-79: Contains conditional Alert components — these are proper children content, not something that should be moved to a `component` prop.
- Line 80-95: Contains an ActionGroup — also proper children content.

The `component` prop on `StackItem` is for specifying the wrapper element type, not for moving children. **No change needed.**

I also notice that line 81 already has `pf-v6-c-form` (already migrated from `pf-v5-c-form`). This confirms previous migration passes have already been applied.

Since all three fixes either don't require changes (the values are already valid, there are no icons in Buttons, and StackItem children shouldn't be moved to `component`), the file is already correct.

Let me verify there are no test files to check:

The test file tests utility functions (`areEnvironmentsChanged`) and has nothing to do with the EnvironmentFormActions component or any PatternFly components. No test changes needed.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (`variant="primary"`, `variant="secondary"`)
**Already correct, no change needed.** The type change only *added* `'stateful'` to the union. The existing values `"primary"` (line 87) and `"secondary"` (line 91) remain valid in the new type.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
**No change needed.** Neither Button instance (lines 82–90 and 91–93) contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. Both Buttons contain only text children (`{t('Save')}` and `{t('Reload')}`), so the icon-to-prop migration does not apply.

### Fix 3 — `StackItem` new `component` prop
**No change needed.** The `component` prop on `StackItem` specifies the wrapper HTML element type. The existing children of both `<StackItem>` instances (conditional Alert blocks at line 58 and the ActionGroup at line 80) are proper JSX children content and should not be moved to the `component` prop.

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (`variant="primary"`, `variant="secondary"`)
**Already correct, no change needed.** The type change only *added* `'stateful'` to the union. The existing values `"primary"` (line 87) and `"secondary"` (line 91) remain valid in the new type.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
**No change needed.** Neither Button instance (lines 82–90 and 91–93) contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. Both Buttons contain only text children (`{t('Save')}` and `{t('Reload')}`), so the icon-to-prop migration does not apply.

### Fix 3 — `StackItem` new `component` prop
